summary: GM subsystem matching slice split from #2617. The PR touched seven loaded src/melee/gm files with small code-shape, declaration-order, typed-field, and JObj API adjustments. The decomp-dev report recorded 2 new matches, gm_80164330 and fn_80185E34, plus 11 unmatched-item improvements in GM functions.

key_files.role: Matched gm_80164330 and improved gm_80164910 by simplifying stage-mask access, ternary return, and replacing an inline unlock-character search/goto with fn_801605EC.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: decomp-dev report lists gm_80164330 as new 100% match and gm_80164910 as improvement

key_files.role: Adjusted local u8 declaration order in gm_8016EDDC while retaining PAD_STACK.
Improved fn_8016FFD4, fn_801701C0, and gm_80173EEC with direct calls, natural indexed loops over six entries, and an index cast for x18 access.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: decomp-dev report lists fn_801701C0 and gm_80173EEC as improvements

key_files.role: Recovered fields in gm_1832_8047368C_t, separated position and scale Vec3 temporaries, and changed fn_80185E34 to access lbl_8047368C fields; reviewer objected to replacing constants with a non-constant global in the same hunk.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: decomp-dev report lists fn_80185E34 as new 100% match
review_comments.md: PsiLupan comment on constant use

key_files.role: Multiple tournament/menu matching improvements: temporary sign handling, declaration ordering, for-loop initialization shape, scale API corrections, HSD_GObj to HSD_JObj access cleanup, branch condition inversion, and pointer iteration over bounce_y.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_8018E46C, fn_8018E85C, gm_801905F0, fn_80198824, fn_80198EBC hunks
text_corpus: decomp-dev report lists five gm_18A5 improvements

key_files.role: Removed a temporary float by inlining JObj translate expressions and changed loop counter declaration type/order in gm_801A9630.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: decomp-dev report lists gm_801A85E4 and gm_801A9630 improvements

key_files.role: Adjusted local declaration ordering and used a separate j loop counter for audio-mask accumulation in gm_8019ECAC_OnEnter.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: decomp-dev report lists gm_8019ECAC_OnEnter as improvement

what_changed: The PR was scoped to src/melee/gm as a directory/subsystem split from #2617 for easier review.

Loaded changed-file metadata shows seven changed files, although the PR body's file list also names gmregclear.c, gmresult.c, and gmresultplayer.c, which are not present in the loaded diff/changed_files slice.
Two functions reached 100% in the decomp-dev report: main/melee/gm/gm_1601 gm_80164330 and main/melee/gm/gm_1832 fn_80185E34.
Several generated-looking pointer/local-variable patterns were replaced with array-indexed loops or direct expressions, especially in fn_801701C0 and fn_80198EBC.
gm_1832_8047368C_t changed from a padding blob after game_type to explicit u8 fields and arrays while preserving sizeof 0x24.

JObj operations were clarified in several places, including using separate Vec3 scale storage in fn_8018325C, scale setters instead of translate setters in fn_8018E85C, and direct gobj->hsd_obj assignment in fn_80198824.

smart_moves.text: Collapsed fn_801701C0's hand-specialized first-entry logic and pointer walkers into two simple indexed loops over six entries, reducing duplicated code while keeping the ranking comparison semantics visible.
Recovered a small local struct layout in gm_1832_8047368C_t instead of continuing to use a single pad array for offsets that are accessed later as fields.
Separated translation and scale temporaries in fn_8018325C, making HSD_JObjGetTranslation/HSD_JObjSetTranslate and HSD_JObjGetScale/HSD_JObjSetScale roles clearer.
In fn_80198EBC, changed repeated da0d0->bounce_y[j] indexed stores to a pointer increment loop, a narrow codegen tactic backed by the PR's reported improvement for the function.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: decomp-dev report lists fn_80198EBC improvement

decomp_lessons.lesson: For repeated per-player/per-slot calculations, try a natural indexed loop before preserving first-element special cases plus pointer walkers.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_801701C0 changed from x58[0] special case plus pointer loops to for k=0..5 and for j=0..5 loops

decomp_lessons.lesson: When an offset blob is used by nearby code as individual bytes and fixed-size arrays, a temporary explicit struct layout can improve reviewability while retaining the static size assertion.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gm_1832_8047368C_t pad_8[0x1C] replaced by xEC/xED/xEE/xEF/xF0/xF1/xF4/xF7/xFA/xFD/x100/pad_1F fields

decomp_lessons.lesson: JObj helper names matter for both semantics and matching: use scale setters for scale values and translation setters for translation values when the diff evidence shows that distinction.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_8018E85C changes HSD_JObjSetTranslateX/Y to HSD_JObjSetScaleX/Y
raw_diff: fn_8018325C uses separate pos and scale Vec3 variables

naming_conventions.text: Recovered struct fields in this slice remain address/offset-style names such as xEF, xF1, xF7, xFD, and x100 rather than semantic names, consistent with conservative naming where meaning is not proven.
Short local role names were introduced only where the role is immediate, such as scale for a Vec3 used with HSD_JObjGetScale/SetScale and bounce_y for a pointer into da0d0->bounce_y.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Declaration order and lifetime steering were used in several functions, including u8 temp_r29/temp_r30 order, TmData initialization placement, float declaration splits, and local loop-counter type/order changes.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gm_801905F0 TmData* tm initialized at declaration
raw_diff: gm_801A9630 changes s32 i to int i and moves declaration

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: A pointer-increment loop was used for bounce_y writes in fn_80198EBC instead of repeated indexed stores.
A ternary return was used in gm_80164330 to collapse an if/return true/false pattern while preserving bool result from a stage_mask test.
A helper call replaced a local search-and-goto sequence in gm_80164910, reducing duplicate control flow around NUM_UNLOCKABLE_CHARACTERS.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gm_80164910 replaces loop/goto found_char with fn_801605EC(internal_id)

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.feedback: Reviewer said to stick with using the constant in the fn_80185E34 hunk.
evidence_refs: review_comments.md: 'As noted on another PR, should stick with using the constant here.'
review_comments.md: hunk shows Player_SetFacingDirection/Player_SetModelScale constants changed in context
review_feedback.interpretation_limits: The loaded review comment does not identify the exact replacement line beyond the hunk context, so do not infer broader reviewer intent beyond preferring the existing constant form here.

follow_up_queries: PR 2621 gm_1832 constant review comment fn_80185E34 lbl_804DA540 0.0f 1.0f
gm_16F1 fn_801701C0 natural indexed loop rankings scores x58
gm_18A5 fn_8018E85C HSD_JObjSetScaleX SetScaleY tournament bracket
gm_1832_8047368C_t xEF xF1 xF7 xFD layout

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR identity, state, changed files, counts
human_pr_text.md: scope, preliminary report, local checks, body file list
raw_diff: complete loaded diff for seven changed files
changed_files.jsonl: seven loaded changed files and added/deleted counts
text_corpus.jsonl: decomp-dev report and review comment
notes: The raw diff and changed_files list are complete for the loaded slice and support file-level change extraction.
The PR body lists ten files, but loaded changed-file metadata and diff show seven changed files; this record treats the seven diff-backed files as changed and records the discrepancy.
The PR body's preliminary full-branch report is explicitly not authoritative for this slice; the decomp-dev issue comment provides the slice-level report used here.

No build logs, objdiff details, or commit discussion beyond the bot report were loaded, so matching-tactic conclusions are based on diff shape plus reported score changes, not independent verification.
The single review comment is terse and should not be generalized beyond its hunk.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Try natural indexed loops before preserving first-entry plus pointer-walker shapes
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2621, fn_801701C0 in src/melee/gm/gm_16F1.c improved by replacing a special x58[0] case and pointer-walking loops with straightforward indexed loops over the same six slots. This supports using natural fixed-count loops for repeated GM slot/ranking logic when the repeated blocks differ only by index.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: decomp-dev report lists fn_801701C0 improvement

accepted_candidate_records.title: Keep uncertain recovered fields offset-style while replacing opaque pads
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2621 replaced gm_1832_8047368C_t's pad_8[0x1C] with explicit offset-style byte fields and arrays while keeping the 0x24 size assert. This is a reviewable middle ground when offsets are proven but semantic names are not.

source_update_candidates.title: Prefer existing constants over equivalent globals or literals when reviewer/local evidence identifies the constant form
source_update_candidates.text: A reviewer on PR #2621 objected in src/melee/gm/gm_1832.c that the code should 'stick with using the constant here' in a hunk where constant-looking calls such as Player_SetFacingDirection(..., 0.0f) and Player_SetModelScale(..., 1.0f) were in context. Curator may consider whether existing standards should explicitly mention preserving local named or literal constants when they are the accepted source form.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential standards refinement, but evidence is a single terse review comment and should be curated cautiously.

source_update_candidates.title: PR #2621 was a GM subsystem split from #2617
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2621 states it was split from #2617 by directory/subsystem with scope src/melee/gm. Loaded diff-backed changes are in gm_1601.c, gm_16AE.c, gm_16F1.c, gm_1832.c, gm_18A5.c, gm_1A4C.c, and gmtou.c.
source_update_candidates.reason: Could help future PR-slice/path-scope search, but the body-vs-diff file-list discrepancy should be preserved.

rejection_notes: Do not record the PR body's ten-file list as the changed-file truth for this slice; only seven files have loaded changed-file and diff evidence.

Do not promote the preliminary full-branch score as this PR slice's validation result; the PR body says per-PR CI/decomp-dev reporting is authoritative, and the loaded bot report gives smaller slice-specific numbers.
Do not infer that all declaration-order changes or PAD_STACK retention are desirable global style; they are matching tactics here and lack detailed objdiff justification in the loaded evidence.
Do not infer semantic meanings for xEC/xEF/xF1/xF7/xFD/x100 fields beyond their recovered offsets and uses.
